review_notes: The functions provided are applicable to Kubernetes and align with the NIST AC-3(3) control, which focuses on access enforcement. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but minor improvements can be made for clarity and consistency. The functions map well to Kubernetes RBAC security controls, ensuring proper access restrictions.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_to_service_account_only' to 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_service_account_specific' for better clarity and consistency with naming patterns.
title: Access Enforcement - De fine the scope of the subjects and objects covered by the policy
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(3) focuses on defining the scope of subjects and objects in access policies. In Kubernetes, this is primarily managed through RBAC, which involves defining roles and bindings to control access to resources. The functions listed ensure that access is restricted to necessary permissions only, avoiding overly broad permissions such as cluster-admin, enforcing least privilege, ensuring role bindings are specific to service accounts, and preventing unnecessary service account token automounting.
